2604.26145.title: Ceci n'est pas une explication: Evaluating Explanation Failures as Explainability Pitfalls in Language Learning Systems
2604.26145.paper_summary: AI-powered language learning tools increasingly provide instant, personalised feedback to millions of learners worldwide. However, this feedback can fail in ways that are difficult for learners--and even teachers--to detect, potentially reinforcing misconceptions and eroding learning outcomes over extended use. We present a portion of L2-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating AI systems in language education that includes (but is not limited to) six critical dimensions of effective feedback: diagnostic accuracy, awareness of appropriacy, causes of error, prioritisation, guidance for improvement, and supporting self-regulation. We analyse how AI systems can fail with respect to these dimensions. These failures, which we argue are conducive to "explainability pitfalls," are AI-generated explanations that appear helpful on the surface but are fundamentally flawed, increasing the risk of attainment, human-AI interaction, and socioaffective harms. We discuss how the specific context of language learning amplifies these risks and outline open questions we believe merit more attention when designing evaluation frameworks specifically. Our analysis aims to expand the community's understanding of both the typology of explainability pitfalls and the contextual dynamics in which they may occur in order to encourage AI developers to better design safe, trustworthy, and effective AI explanations.
2604.26145.paper_summary_zh: AI 驅動的語言學習工具越來越多地為全球數百萬學習者提供即時的個性化反饋。<lb/>然而，這些反饋可能以學習者——甚至教師——難以察覺的方式失效，潛在地強化誤解並在長期使用中侵蝕學習成果。<lb/>我們展示了 L2-Bench 的一部分，這是一個用於評估語言教育中 AI 系統的基準，涵蓋（但不限於）六個有效反饋的關鍵維度：診斷準確性、適當性意識、錯誤原因、優先排序、改進指導和支持自我調節。<lb/>我們分析了 AI 系統在這些維度上可能失效的情況。<lb/>這些失敗，我們認為有助於「可解釋性陷阱」，是 AI 生成的解釋，表面上看似有幫助，但根本上存在缺陷，增加了獲得成就、人機互動和社會情感傷害的風險。<lb/>我們討論了語言學習的具體背景如何放大這些風險，並概述了我們認為在設計評估框架時值得更多關注的開放性問題。<lb/>我們的分析旨在擴展社群對可解釋性陷阱的類型學以及可能發生的背景動態的理解，以鼓勵 AI 開發者更好地設計安全、值得信賴和有效的 AI 解釋。
2604.26145.author: Ben Knight et.al.
2604.26145.authors: Ben Knight, Wm. Matthew Kennedy, James Edgell
